text:
MECO Engineering is celebrating its 40th anniversary this year. This long-spanning career has been built on serving municipalities, such as yours, in a vast span of projects with varying needs, requirements, funding, and challenges. We take pride in gaining the majority of our business from repeat, satisfied customers.

MECO currently has six office locations, with a location conveniently close for all your project needs in Hannibal, MO, approximately 13 miles from the City of Palmyra. This close proximity, as well as our knowledge of the area and ever-changing regulations, and our personal experience with the City, allows us to assure you of our ability to serve the City in a high-quality, cost-effective manner.

MECO's "Standard of Care" and "Project Approach" is a team-centric based philosophy to ensure that every step of project scope development and design review fully invests the client in each step of the process. MECO does not believe in an engineer-driven project development process; we see the most value in investing our client input in the process, first and foremost. Our clients will never see MECO force a project or a solution upon them. Our clients will always lead the initiative for developing projects and working with our professional staff to determine the best suited solutions to their needs.

MECO is a leader throughout Missouri and Central Illinois in the specialized discipline of potable water engineering. Our professionals provide sound engineering solutions for groundwater and surface water supply and treatment requirements to meet regulatory compliance; distribution systems, and storage needs. Our clients include municipalities, rural water districts, co-operatives, wholesale water commissions, educational institutions, the State of Missouri, and clients in the private sector.

Set to go to bid in June of 2025 is a newly formed regional water system with Illinois Alluvial Regional Water Company. This is a $210M project serving 7 members with an 8MGD wellfield, 3 miles of 30" raw water transmission main, a new 8 MGD water treatment plant, 2 1 MG clearwells, 2 MG composite elevated water tower and 50 miles of finished water transmission main. $66 million in government funding was applied for and granted for this project.

As MECO's Licensed Structural Engineer (SE-Illinois), Mr. Bensman leads the Structural Department and is responsible for structural projects undertaken by the firm. He is also a Civil and Municipal Engineer. Mr. Bensman began his career with MECO in 1997 as an engineer intern. He worked up to a Vice President/Principal Engineer and has now taken on a new role as a Senior Principal Engineer.

His engineering responsibilities include project management, design, bridge engineering, construction management; structural and hydraulic design/analysis, structure load-rating, hydraulic and bridge report and cost estimate preparation for structural projects. Mr. Bensman comes with a wide range of experience from works on highway bridges, streets, roads and highways, water and waste water treatment plants, industrial facilities, storm water facilities, retaining walls, and municipal buildings. He is qualified to perform ultrasonic testing procedures on steel structures, including thickness testing/subsurface defect detection.
